envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Slack connector readiness substrate per ADR-0084. RULE 21 research arc input for C2 first-real-connector decision. Wave 6 matrix score 16.00 (top of Collaboration TIER_1). Read-first by default; write actions disabled by default; risky writes approval-gated; map-region approval required.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Readiness catalog informs implementation. Activation still requires Foundation governance authorization + map-region approval + per-connector RULE 21 research arc completion. Suggest-only — first real connector decision remains Founder-gated at C2.
audit_expectations: INVOKE_CONNECTOR + ACTION_* at future C2+ runtime per ADR-0084

items.MCP_posture: Slack official MCP server exists in the broader MCP ecosystem; substrate-honest evaluation deferred to C2 pre-flight to compare maintenance posture against direct API integration. Default initial path is direct API + read-first scopes; MCP layer can be added if it reduces governance / auth surface complexity.
items.OAuth_model: OAuth 2.0 v2 flow via oauth.v2.access. Bot tokens (xoxb-*) tied to app installation per workspace, not to individual user identity — preferred for read-first operation per Slack docs because the app remains installed even when the installing user is deactivated. User tokens (xoxp-*) only for user-context operations.
items.app_installation_model: Modern Slack apps (NOT classic apps — classic apps deprecated November 2026 per Slack docs). Per-workspace installation; Enterprise Grid installations support org-wide scopes for admin operations.
items.admin_consent_model: Workspace owners or admins consent at OAuth install time. admin.* scopes require Workspace Owner or higher. Enterprise Grid admin scopes (e.g., admin.legalHolds.*) granted at the enterprise level and tied to Org Owner; revoked if the installer's role drops below Org Owner.
read_capabilities: channels.history (public channel messages — explicit per-channel scope)
conversations.list (public + private channels the bot is a member of)
conversations.history (messages from channels the bot is a member of)
write_capabilities: chat.postMessage (send messages — disabled by default; only after admin approval + governance review)
files.upload (file upload — disabled by default; PII / payload-leak risk)
conversations.invite / conversations.create (channel management — disabled by default)
reactions.add (emoji reactions — lower risk; still approval-gated)
webhook_or_event_capabilities: Slack Events API (subscribe to message.channels, app_mention, channel_created, etc.) — preferred over polling
Webhook URL with HMAC signing-secret verification (compose against existing verifyInboundHmac Hardening Wave B substrate)
Socket Mode alternative for development; HTTP Events API for production
Event subscriptions require Bot Token Scopes + Event Subscriptions enabled in app manifest
risky_write_actions: chat.postMessage to broad audience (e.g., #general or all-org channels)
DM sends to high-trust recipients (executives, board, legal)
required_approval_gates: Map-region approval per ADR-0082 Amendment 1 cartographer doctrine (Tool Map + Workflow Map regions must include Slack)
Admin policy approval at activation per ADR-0084
Per-call governance approval for any write action
Workflow purpose binding per ADR-0081 (Stage 3+ proposed-Action invocations only)
dual_control_recommendations: Bulk write operations (>10 messages in a window) — second-actor approval recommended
External-facing channel posts — dual-control recommended for shared channels
App reinstall / scope expansion — admin dual-control recommended
DMW_scope_implications: Slack messages read-first: provenance tagged channel-scoped + sender-role-scoped per Wave 2.1 §25 DMW scope notes
DMs treated as user-self-scoped DMW only; never visible to other Twins / Admin Twin
Per ADR-0046 dual-context: Personal AI Agent twin Slack reads → PERSONAL wallet; Enterprise AI Agent Slack reads → ENTERPRISE wallet
Shared / external channels treated as cross-org DMW boundary — additional governance review required
Sensitive channels (legal / HR / M&A) excluded by default until Risk Map explicit approval
workflow_purpose_bindings: ADR-0081 Stage 2 recommendation: Slack catch-up summaries (per-role first-week aha)
ADR-0081 Stage 3 proposed-Action: Drafted Slack replies / reactions awaiting human approval
ADR-0081 Stage 4 governed execution: Auto-replies under explicit delegated authority + audit
Slack as workflow trigger: incoming messages → workflow recommendations (read-only inbound)
items.billing_pack_mapping: Collaboration Pack family per ADR-0083 Amendment 1 §9.4 (connector-pack.collaboration.v1).
Dandelion_map_dependencies: Workflow Map must approve Slack-bound workflows by purpose
Authority Map must approve write-action authority (per-role / dual-control)
Memory / DMW Map must approve Slack-derived memory categories + scope
Risk Map must clear Slack (no map-blocking risk)
audit_expectations: INVOKE_CONNECTOR + ACTION_* lifecycle at C2+ runtime (existing literals; no new audit literal required per ADR-0084)
Webhook ingestion audited via existing verifyInboundHmac path (existing literals preserved)
Admin kill-switch toggles audited via existing ADMIN_ACTION discriminators
secret_handling_requirements: Slack signing secret stored via per-tenant secret_ref env-var-NAME pattern per ADR-0024 + ADR-0019 — never raw secret at rest in repo or logs
Bot token (xoxb-*) per workspace stored via ConnectorBinding.secret_ref (existing substrate at apps/api/src/services/connector/ConnectorBinding model)
OAuth client ID / client secret per environment via env-var-NAMEs only; deployment-target secret manager resolves payload
Token rotation discipline: when Slack rotates / reissues a bot token, ConnectorBinding rotation flow must invalidate cached payload + emit ADMIN_ACTION:SECRET_ROTATED
no_leak_rules: Connector payloads (full message content, file content, attachment metadata) never traverse responses outside the wallet's scope
Bot token / signing secret never appears in logs / error messages / no-leak guard surfaces
User Slack IDs (Uxxx) never exposed cross-tenant
Channel IDs and names never exposed cross-tenant
Search query text never reflected in error envelopes per no-leak guard precedent
tenant_isolation_requirements: Slack workspace per tenant — never shared OAuth app across tenants without explicit Founder authorization
Per-tenant ConnectorBinding rows enforce wallet_id + entity scope
Same-org boundary absolute per ADR-0049 + ADR-0027
Enterprise Grid installations require tenant-to-Grid-workspace mapping recorded at activation
items.rate_limit_notes: Slack uses Web API tier-based rate limits (Tier 1 = 1 req/min, Tier 4 = 100+ req/min); chat.postMessage = Tier 4 (~1 req/sec per channel). Events API has separate workspace-level event delivery throughput. Defensive: implement exponential backoff + jitter + tier-aware queueing per workspace.
testing_strategy: Fixture-based read tests against FixtureBasedConnectorAdapter pattern (mirrors FixtureBasedLLMProvider per ADR-0014)
Replay-mode integration tests using recorded Slack API responses (no live workspace required in CI)
Webhook signature verification tests against verifyInboundHmac existing substrate (Hardening Wave B)
Tenant-isolation assertion tests: cross-tenant ConnectorBinding access must 403
No-leak guard tests: payloads + tokens + signing secret never surface in response / audit
Rate-limit retry tests using fixture provider returning 429 + Retry-After
implementation_risks: Classic-apps deprecation Nov 2026: implementation must target modern Slack apps from day one
Confused-deputy: bot acting in shared channels could leak between Slack workspaces — GOVSEC.6 hardening recommended before C2
Bot reinstall flow on installer-role demotion (Enterprise Grid): token revocation cascade must be handled gracefully
Search scopes vs message scopes: search.messages requires user-token (xoxp-) which raises sovereignty / scope tradeoffs
Events API delivery is best-effort; outage recovery requires replay strategy
items.first_slice_recommendation: C2 Slack read-first connector runtime — bot-token-based reads of public channels + bot-member private channels + events ingestion via verifyInboundHmac. NO writes at C2. NO DM reads at C2 (forward-substrate). NO user-token (xoxp-) search at C2. Write actions deferred to C6 per ADR-0084 9-slice ladder. Recommend GOVSEC.6 (agent abuse / confused-deputy hardening) before or alongside C2.
